7. Test case T-6: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with an in-band ring tone.
8. Test case T-7: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG with an in-band ring tone.
9. Test case T-8: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with no user action required.
10. Test case T-9: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG with no user action required.
11. Test case T-10: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with a user action on the HS.
12. Test case T-11: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG with a user action on the AG.
13. Test case T-12: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with a user action on the HS and no ring tone.
14. Test case T-13: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG with a user action on the HS and no ring tone.
15. Test case T-14: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with a user action on the HS and a ring tone.
16. Test case T-15: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG with a user action on the HS and a ring tone.

These test cases cover the requirement for the audio connection transfer from HS to AG, including the ability for the AG to initiate the connection, the HS to accept the connection, and the AG to release the connection. Additionally, the test cases cover the ability for the AG to provide ring tones during connection establishment and the HS to give ring tones during connection release. The test cases also cover the ability for the user to initiate the connection release on either the HS or the AG, and the ability for the user to initiate the connection release on the HS or the AG with a ring tone.

The following is a list of test cases that cover the requirement for remote audio volume control in the HS and AG:

1. Test case T-0: The AG can control the gain of the microphone or speaker of the HS by sending unsolicited result codes +VGM or +VGS respectively. The HS stores the VGS and VGM settings at connection release and restores them using AT+VGS and AT+VGM. The AG initiates a call/audio connection to the HS from a test device and the HS establishes the connection by the corresponding user action.
2. Test case T-1: The AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG. The HS alerts the user upon establishing the connection and the user action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.
3. Test case T-2: The AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ring tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG. The HS alerts the user upon alerting in the HS earpiece and the user action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.
4. Test case T-3: The AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ring tone during connection establishment initiated from the HS. The HS alerts the user upon alerting in the HS earpiece and the user action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.
5. Test case T-4: The AG can accept a connection release initiated from the HS. The HS initiates the user action on the HS to establish the connection and the AG accepts the connection.
6. Test case T-5: The HS can accept a connection release initiated from the AG. The AG initiates the user action or internal event to release the connection and the HS establishes the connection.
7. Test case T-6: The AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ring tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG. The HS alerts the user upon alerting in the HS earpiece and the user action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.
8. Test case T-7: The HS can accept a connection release initiated from the AG. The AG initiates the user action or internal event to release the connection and the HS establishes the connection.
9. Test case T-8: The AG can accept a connection release initiated from the HS. The HS initiates the user action on the HS to release the connection and the AG establishes the connection.
10. Test case T-9: The AG can accept a connection release initiated from the HS. The HS initiates the user action on the HS to release the connection and the AG establishes the connection.
11. Test case T-10: The HS can accept an audio connection transfer from the AG initiated by a user action on the HS. The HS returns to Standby mode after the transfer.
12. Test case T-11: The AG can accept an audio connection transfer from the HS initiated by a user action on the AG. The HS returns to Standby mode after the transfer.
13. Test case T-12: The HS can accept an audio connection transfer from the AG initiated by a user action on the AG and the HS returns to Standby mode.
14. Test case T-13: The HS can accept an audio connection transfer from the AG initiated by a user action on the AG and the HS returns to Standby mode after the transfer. The HS stores the speaker volume settings at connection release and restores them upon establishing a new connection. The user action on the HS transfers the audio connection from the AG to the HS.
15. Test case T-14: The HS can accept an audio connection transfer from the AG initiated by a user action on the AG and the HS returns to Standby mode after the transfer. The HS stores the speaker volume settings at connection release and restores them upon establishing a new connection. The user action on the HS transfers the audio connection from the AG to the HS and sets the speaker volume to the maximum or minimum level.
